summary.rationale: Theme direction is now documented as a workflow protocol instead of custom globals.css variables, and the affected pages render cleanly on desktop and mobile after migration.

findings.message: The pages now use default shadcn semantic tokens, so the visual tone is less warm than before. This is acceptable for removing CSS-encoded theme state, but a later profile application task may tune the shadcn semantic variables.
findings.recommendation: If the new Product Craft Workbench profile needs a more distinctive runtime expression, run a separate browser-verified token task.

notes: Mobile checks reported no horizontal overflow for /songs or /quiz.
Removed CSS variables and selectors are absent from source and served CSS.
Songs list, song detail, and quiz routes remain readable and scannable.
The quiz primary action remains visually obvious.
The songs detail lead-sheet rows render and remain legible.
The new theme-usage protocol properly makes profile selection a workflow concern.
The migration avoids hiding theme decisions in globals.css.
The visual tone is functional but intentionally not final brand styling.
The implementation follows Product Craft Workbench at the workflow level by preferring task clarity and semantic utilities over decorative global theme variables.

next_steps: Consider a separate visual profile application task if the user wants shadcn semantic variables tuned to a more distinctive Product Craft Workbench runtime look.
